title: lomatium idahoense
intro: Lomatium idahoense, also known as Idaho biscuit root, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone compounds. The plant is said to offer health benefits such as relieving symptoms of the common cold and flu, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Lomatium idahoense include tinctures, teas, and infusions, which can be made from the roots and stems of the plant, although caution is advised due to potential allergic reactions and interactions with other medications.
